subject: chemistry
name_zh: IB DP 化学（2025 新大纲）
description: Based on the new IB DP Chemistry syllabus (first teaching 2023, first assessment 2025). Organized by two concepts: Structure (1.x) and Reactivity (2.x / 3.x).

domains.name: 结构 1 · 物质的模型 Structure 1 · Models of the Particulate Nature of Matter
domains.name_en: Structure 1 · Models of the Particulate Nature of Matter

nodes.name: 物质的粒子本质与物态
curriculum_points:

nodes.name: 原子结构（电子构型/同位素）
curriculum_points: Atomic structure

nodes.name: 量子力学的原子模型（HL）
curriculum_points: Describe the quantum mechanical model of the atom, including the concepts of atomic orbitals, quantum numbers (n, l, m_l, m_s), and electron spin, and use it to explain atomic emission spectra and ionization energies (AO2, AO3).

Apply the Aufbau principle, Hund's rule, and the Pauli exclusion principle to deduce electron configurations for atoms and ions up to Z = 36, and explain the anomalous configurations of Cr and Cu (AO2).

domains.name: 结构 2 · 化学键与结构 Structure 2 · The Molecular World

nodes.name: 离子键
curriculum_points: Explain the formation of ionic bonds between metals and non-metals as a result of electron transfer, leading to the formation of cations and anions held together by electrostatic forces (AO2).

Deduce the formula and name of an ionic compound from the charges on the constituent ions, and describe the typical physical properties (high melting point, brittleness, electrical conductivity) of ionic compounds in relation to their lattice structure (AO2, AO3).

nodes.name: 共价键（VSEPR/极性/杂化）
curriculum_points: Describe the formation of a covalent bond by the sharing of a pair of electrons between atoms, and represent it using Lewis (electron dot) structures for molecules and ions containing up to six electron pairs on each atom (AO2).

Explain the shapes of, and bond angles in, simple molecules and ions with up to six electron pairs (including lone pairs) on the central atom using the valence shell electron pair repulsion (VSEPR) theory (AO2, AO3).

nodes.name: 金属键
curriculum_points: Describe the metallic bond as the electrostatic attraction between a lattice of positive ions and delocalized electrons, and use this model to explain the typical physical properties of metals, such as malleability, ductility, and electrical and thermal conductivity (AO2).
Explain the trends in melting points of metals across a period and down a group in terms of the strength of the metallic bond, relating it to ionic radius and charge (AO3).

nodes.name: 分子间作用力（HL：伦敦/偶极/氢键）
curriculum_points:
c (cid:31) f(cid:30)
E =hf

Speed of light in vacuum c 3.00 × 108 m s−1
Planck constant h 6.63 × 10−34 J s

domains.name: 结构 3 · 周期表与分类 Structure 3 · Classification of Matter

nodes.name: 周期表与周期性趋势
curriculum_points: Periodic table & trends

nodes.name: 有机化学官能团与命名
curriculum_points: Identify and name organic compounds containing up to six carbon atoms and one or more of the following functional groups: alkene, alkyne, halogenoalkane, alcohol, aldehyde, ketone, carboxylic acid, ester, amine, nitrile, amide, and arene, using IUPAC nomenclature (AO1).
Deduce structural formulas and apply IUPAC rules to name isomers of non-cyclic organic compounds containing up to six carbon atoms and one or more functional groups (AO2).

nodes.name: 光谱分析（HL：IR/NMR/MS）
curriculum_points: Analyze and interpret data from mass spectrometry (MS) to determine the relative molecular mass of a compound and identify fragments to gain information about structure (AO2, AO3).

Deduce the structure of organic compounds from information provided by a combination of analytical techniques, including high resolution 1H NMR (number of signals, chemical shift, integration, and splitting patterns), infrared (IR) spectroscopy, and mass spectrometry (AO2, AO3).

domains.name: 反应性 1 · 热力学 Reactivity 1 · What Drives Chemical Reactions

nodes.name: 焓变与反应热
curriculum_points: Define and apply the terms standard enthalpy change of reaction (ΔH°), formation (ΔH_f°), combustion (ΔH_c°), and neutralization (AO1).
Calculate the heat energy change (q) for a reaction using the expression q = mcΔT, and apply calorimetry data to determine the enthalpy change per mole for a reaction (AO2, AO3).

nodes.name: Hess 定律与键能
curriculum_points: State Hess's Law and use it to calculate enthalpy changes from provided data, including constructing and using enthalpy cycles (AO2).
Calculate approximate enthalpy changes from average bond enthalpy data, and explain why such values may differ from those determined using Hess's Law (AO2, AO3).

nodes.name: 熵与自由能（HL）
curriculum_points: Predict whether a reaction will be spontaneous by calculating the standard entropy change, ΔS°, for the system and the standard Gibbs free energy change, ΔG°, using the equation ΔG° = ΔH° - TΔS° (AO2).
Explain the relationship between the total entropy change (ΔS_total), the feasibility of a reaction, and the temperature at which a reaction becomes spontaneous (AO3).

domains.name: 反应性 2 · 化学变化 Reactivity 2 · How Much, How Fast, How Far
domains.name_en: Reactivity 2 · How Much, How Fast, How Far

nodes.name: 化学计量学（mol/方程式/产率）
curriculum_points: Apply the mole concept to substances, using the relationships n = m/M, n = V (gas)/V_m, and n = cV to determine amounts of reactants and products in chemical reactions (AO2).
Determine the limiting reactant and the theoretical yield of a reaction from given data, and calculate percentage yield and atom economy (AO2, AO3).

nodes.name: 反应动力学（速率/活化能/催化剂）
curriculum_points: Describe the factors that affect the rate of a chemical reaction (concentration, pressure, temperature, surface area, catalyst) in terms of collision theory (AO2).

Analyze concentration-time and rate-concentration graphs to deduce zero-, first-, and second-order reactions, and determine the rate expression and the value of the rate constant, k, from initial rates data (AO2, AO3).

nodes.name: 化学平衡（Le Chatelier/Kc/Kp）
curriculum_points: State the characteristics of a system in dynamic equilibrium, and deduce the equilibrium constant expression, K_c, from the equation for a homogeneous reaction (AO1, AO2).

Apply Le Châtelier's principle to predict the qualitative effects of changes in concentration, pressure, and temperature on the position of equilibrium and on the value of the equilibrium constant, explaining the choice of conditions in industrial processes (AO2, AO3).

domains.name: 反应性 3 · 电子转移 Reactivity 3 · Electron Transfer & Proton Transfer
domains.name_en: Reactivity 3 · What are the mechanisms of chemical change

nodes.name: 酸碱理论（pH/Ka/Kb/缓冲液）
curriculum_points: Define acids and bases according to the Brønsted–Lowry theory, and identify conjugate acid-base pairs in proton transfer reactions (AO1, AO2).

Distinguish between strong and weak acids and bases in terms of the extent of dissociation, and calculate the pH, pOH, [H+], and [OH-] for strong acids and bases and weak acids given appropriate data (AO2, AO3).

nodes.name: 氧化还原与电化学
curriculum_points:
c = fλ
E = hf

pH = −log [H O+]
10 3
or
pH = −log [H+]

K = [H+] [OH−]
w

nodes.name: 有机反应机理（HL：SN1/SN2/E1/E2）
curriculum_points: Describe and explain the mechanisms of nucleophilic substitution reactions (S_N1 and S_N2) and electrophilic addition reactions, using curly arrows to show the movement of electron pairs (AO2, AO3).

Predict the major and minor products of organic reactions, including those involving regioselectivity (Markovnikov's rule) and stereochemistry, and explain the formation of stereoisomers in certain addition and substitution reactions (AO2, AO3).
